name: VA2XP
description: Voltage-controlled 4-channel stereo enhancer-mixer and output module with cue/headphone amp. Channel 1 combines stereo VC crossfade and VC pan with independent CVs, channels 2-3 add VC pan to stereo pairs, and channel 4 is a stereo level input.

controls.name: Channel 1 Level / Crossfade
controls.description: Sets intensity and level of the crossfader between the L and R inputs.

controls.name: Channel 1 Pan
controls.description: Places the crossfade mix toward left or right of the stereo field.

controls.name: Channel 2 Pan
controls.description: Manual pan position for channel 2's stereo pair.

controls.name: Channel 3 Pan
controls.description: Manual pan position for channel 3's stereo pair.

controls.name: Channel 4 Level
controls.description: Manual level for the stereo input channel (no CV, no pan).

controls.name: Cue / Mix Button
controls.description: Toggles whether the headphone output monitors the Cue input or the main Mix.

controls.name: Headphone Level
controls.description: Sets the headphone amplifier output level.

inputs.name: Channel 1 In L
inputs.description: Left (or mono) input for channel 1. If only L is patched, the module creates a stereo image controllable by pan CV.

inputs.name: Channel 1 In R
inputs.description: Right input for channel 1. With both L and R patched, channel 1 acts as a VC crossfader between the two sources.

inputs.name: Channel 1 Crossfade CV
inputs.description: CV that moves the crossfade position between L and R on channel 1, independently of pan CV.

inputs.name: Channel 1 Pan CV
inputs.description: CV that places the crossfade result left or right in the stereo field; polarity/strength determines distance from center.

inputs.name: Channel 2 In L
inputs.description: Left (or mono) input for channel 2's stereo pair.

inputs.name: Channel 2 In R
inputs.description: Right input for channel 2. Under pan CV the L and R sides twist across the stereo field.

inputs.name: Channel 2 Pan CV
inputs.description: CV control of channel 2's stereo pan.

inputs.name: Channel 3 In L
inputs.description: Left (or mono) input for channel 3's stereo pair.

inputs.name: Channel 3 In R
inputs.description: Right input for channel 3. Under pan CV the L and R sides twist across the stereo field.

inputs.name: Channel 3 Pan CV
inputs.description: CV control of channel 3's stereo pan.

inputs.name: Channel 4 In L
inputs.description: Left (or mono) input for channel 4, a level-only stereo channel with no pan and no CV.

inputs.name: Cue In
inputs.description: Dedicated cue input that can be auditioned on headphones without reaching the main outputs when the front-panel button is set to Cue.

outputs.name: Main Out L
outputs.description: Main stereo output, left channel. Sum of all four channels.

outputs.name: Main Out R
outputs.description: Main stereo output, right channel. Sum of all four channels.

outputs.name: Headphones
outputs.description: Stereo headphone amplifier output; monitors Mix or Cue depending on the button state.

behaviors.name: Independent crossfade and pan on channel 1
behaviors.trigger: channel 1 used with separate Crossfade CV and Pan CV
behaviors.description: The two CVs do different jobs at the same time: Crossfade CV moves the balance between L and R sources, while Pan CV places that result in the stereo field. You can modulate one, both, or neither.

behaviors.name: Mono-to-stereo behavior on channels 1-3
behaviors.trigger: only the L input of channels 1, 2 or 3 is patched
behaviors.description: With a single mono source patched to L, the channel becomes a VC-level stereo channel whose position in the field is controlled by the pan CV and/or pan knob.

behaviors.name: Stereo twist on channels 2 and 3
behaviors.trigger: channels 2 or 3 receive a stereo pair and pan CV
behaviors.description: Unlike a standard pan law, the left and right sides move/twist against each other as CV moves, producing a motion image rather than a simple balance shift.

behaviors.name: Channel 4 as stereo return
behaviors.trigger: stereo source patched into channel 4 L and R
behaviors.description: Channel 4 is a clean stereo level stage with no pan and no CV, making it the natural place for a bus return, click track or premixed stem.

behaviors.name: Independent cue monitoring
behaviors.description: The signal on Cue In is routed only to the headphone amp, never into the Main Outs, so you can audition sources privately during a live set.

behaviors.name: Per-channel LED metering
behaviors.description: Front-panel LEDs indicate activity on every channel, so gain staging and CV-driven pan movement are visible even when the mix is feeding headphones or a downstream mixer.

patchIdeas.name: Two-source VC crossfader (channel 1)
patchIdeas.patch: Use channel 1 as a dedicated VC crossfader between two voices, with pan knob centered so the blend sits in the middle of the stereo field.
steps: Patch Voice A into VA2XP Channel 1 In L.
Patch Voice B into VA2XP Channel 1 In R.
Patch a slow LFO or envelope into VA2XP Channel 1 Crossfade CV.
Leave Channel 1 Pan CV unpatched; set Pan knob to center (12 o'clock).
Turn Channel 1 Level/Crossfade knob to about 12 o'clock to set crossfade depth.
Patch VA2XP Main Out L and Main Out R to your monitors or recorder.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Main Out L/R — the two voices morphing smoothly into each other under CV control.
patchIdeas.hear: Voice A fades out as Voice B fades in, following the LFO/envelope. Turning the Level/Crossfade knob toward either side biases the blend; opening Pan CV adds stereo motion on top.
patchIdeas.diagram:
VA2XP:

Voice A:
Voice B:
LFO:
Output:

- Voice A (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In L)
- Voice B (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In R)
- LFO (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 1 Crossfade CV)
- VA2XP (Main Out L) -> Output (L)
- VA2XP (Main Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Mono-to-stereo spread
patchIdeas.patch: Patch one mono voice to channel 1 L only and use the pan CV to turn it into a wide, moving stereo image.
steps: Patch a mono VCO (or full voice) into VA2XP Channel 1 In L only; leave In R unpatched.
Patch a slow triangle LFO into VA2XP Channel 1 Pan CV.
Leave Channel 1 Crossfade CV unpatched.
Set Channel 1 Level/Crossfade to about 12 o'clock so the mono source is at full level.
Start with Channel 1 Pan knob centered, then nudge it while the LFO runs.
Patch VA2XP Main Out L/R to your monitors.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Main Out L/R — the mono voice now moving across a stereo field.
patchIdeas.hear: The voice drifts left and right with the LFO. Faster LFO = a classic autopanner; slower LFO = long, cinematic width breathing. Try an envelope as CV to sweep per note.
patchIdeas.diagram:
VA2XP:

VCO:
LFO:
Output:

- VCO (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In L)
- LFO (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 1 Pan CV)
- VA2XP (Main Out L) -> Output (L)
- VA2XP (Main Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice animated panorama
patchIdeas.patch: Four voices across channels 1-4, three of them animated by different modulation sources so the mix constantly shifts in the stereo field.
steps: Patch Voice 1 into Channel 1 In L (mono), Voice 2 into Channel 2 In L, Voice 3 into Channel 3 In L, Voice 4 stereo into Channel 4 In L and In R.
Patch slow LFO 1 into Channel 1 Pan CV.
Patch slow LFO 2 into Channel 2 Pan CV.
Patch a random or S&H source into Channel 3 Pan CV.
Set all pan knobs to center; set channel 4 Level to taste to anchor the mix.
Patch VA2XP Main Out L/R to your monitors.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Main Out L/R — a four-voice mix where three voices pan on their own schedules while channel 4 stays locked as the center anchor.
patchIdeas.hear: A living stereo field: each pan CV has a different rate/shape so voices never sweep in the same direction at once. Channel 4 keeps the mix grounded so the motion feels musical rather than chaotic.
patchIdeas.diagram:
VA2XP:

Voice 1:
Voice 2:
Voice 3:
Voice 4:
LFO 1:
LFO 2:
Random:
Output:

- Voice 1 (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In L)
- Voice 2 (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 2 In L)
- Voice 3 (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 3 In L)
- Voice 4 (Out L) -> VA2XP (Channel 4 In L)
- Voice 4 (Out R) -> VA2XP (Channel 4 In R)
- LFO 1 (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 1 Pan CV)
- LFO 2 (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 2 Pan CV)
- Random (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 3 Pan CV)
- VA2XP (Main Out L) -> Output (L)
- VA2XP (Main Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Cue preview bus for live sets
patchIdeas.patch: Keep the audience mix on Main Out L/R while you audition the next patch privately on headphones using the Cue input.
steps: Patch Drums into Channel 2 In L/R and Synth into Channel 3 In L/R so they're in the main mix.
Patch the source you want to preview (a new voice, a click track, or a cued loop) into VA2XP Cue In.
Press the Cue/Mix button so it's set to Cue.
Raise the Headphone Level knob until you can hear the cue source clearly.
Patch VA2XP Main Out L/R to the PA and VA2XP Headphones to your headphones.
Press the button back to Mix when you want to monitor the audience mix instead.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Main Out L/R — the audience/house mix; Headphones — the Cue source (or the mix) depending on the button.
patchIdeas.hear: The PA keeps playing unchanged while you solo the cued signal in your headphones. Flip the button and the headphones snap back to the full mix — perfect for DJ-style previewing during a modular set.
patchIdeas.diagram:
VA2XP:

Drums:
Synth:
Click:
PA:
Phones:

- Drums (Out L) -> VA2XP (Channel 2 In L)
- Drums (Out R) -> VA2XP (Channel 2 In R)
- Synth (Out L) -> VA2XP (Channel 3 In L)
- Synth (Out R) -> VA2XP (Channel 3 In R)
- Click (Out) -> VA2XP (Cue In)
- VA2XP (Main Out L) -> PA (L)
- VA2XP (Main Out R) -> PA (R)
- VA2XP (Headphones) -> Phones (In)

patchIdeas.name: Crossfade + pan on the same voice
patchIdeas.patch: Exploit channel 1's unique dual-CV layout: modulate the L/R crossfade with an envelope while a separate LFO pans the result.
steps: Patch a bright VCO into Channel 1 In L and a dark filtered version of the same VCO into Channel 1 In R.
Patch an envelope (triggered per note) into Channel 1 Crossfade CV so each note opens from dark to bright.
Patch a slow sine LFO into Channel 1 Pan CV for ambient left/right drift.
Set Channel 1 Level/Crossfade to 12 o'clock and Pan knob to center.
Patch VA2XP Main Out L/R to your monitors.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Main Out L/R — a single voice whose tone morphs per note while its position drifts in the field.
patchIdeas.hear: Every note opens dark-to-bright as the envelope crossfades the two timbres, and the whole voice slowly sweeps across the stereo image on the LFO. Two dimensions of motion from one mixer channel.
patchIdeas.diagram:
VA2XP:

VCO bright:
VCO dark:
Env:
LFO:
Output:

- VCO bright (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In L)
- VCO dark (Out) -> VA2XP (Channel 1 In R)
- Env (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 1 Crossfade CV)
- LFO (Out) >> VA2XP (Channel 1 Pan CV)
- VA2XP (Main Out L) -> Output (L)
- VA2XP (Main Out R) -> Output (R)
